name: Jafari No-Interest Credit Union
description_short: Houston-based Islamic credit union offering 0% interest loans and NCUA-insured savings accounts to Jafari Muslim community members across the US.
description_long:
Jafari No-Interest Credit Union (JNICU) is a federally chartered, NCUA-insured credit union based in Houston, Texas. Approved by the Texas Credit Union Department and the National Credit Union Administration in early 2016, it was established by and for the Jafari Muslim community. The credit union operates according to Islamic finance principles, which prohibit the payment or receipt of interest (riba), drawing guidance from the Quran and the teachings of Prophet Muhammad. Rather than charging interest on loans, JNICU sustains operations through member account fees, voluntary contributions, and donations, keeping annual operating costs at approximately $40,000 through donated office space and an all-volunteer staff.

JNICU offers two core product lines: a federally insured savings account and a suite of no-interest loan products. The savings account requires a $200 opening deposit with a $50 minimum balance; all deposits and withdrawals are handled by ACH transfer only — no cash, ATM, debit card, or checking services are available. Loan offerings include refinancing for existing auto, student, or home loans up to $35,000; home and car repair, medical, and college loans from $1,200 to $8,000; personal loans from $3,000 to $8,000; and business equipment purchase or repair loans from $5,000 to $16,000. Non-refundable loan application fees of $25 or $50 apply, and all borrowers must meet income, credit, and other eligibility requirements.

JNICU is one of the very few NCUA-chartered credit unions in the United States that charges zero interest on every loan product. As of December 2025, the credit union had 365 members, $2.115 million in deposits, and had issued $3.634 million in total loans since inception — saving members a cumulative $1.155 million in interest charges. Membership is restricted to attendees of specific Jafari community centers in Houston, Dallas, and Austin, or their qualifying family members, who may live anywhere in the US. The CU currently serves members in more than a dozen states through these family-based membership relationships.

JNICU fills a genuine and underserved need for interest-free financial services within the Jafari Muslim community. The no-interest model is structural and permanent, not a promotional rate. That said, the institution is small, with limited loan caps, no checking or ATM services, and a narrowly defined membership base tied to specific community centers. Monthly fees ranging from $3 to $60 — determined by the ratio of a member's savings balance to outstanding loans — replace interest as the revenue mechanism and are mandatory. For eligible members, particularly those carrying high-interest auto or student loans, JNICU represents a compelling, Sharia-compliant alternative to conventional lending. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Charges 0% interest on all loan products — a permanent feature, not a promotional rate
NCUA-insured deposits up to $250,000, providing full federal deposit protection
Has saved members a cumulative $1.155 million in interest since inception (as of Dec 2025)
Annual operating expenses held to ~$40,000 through donated office space and volunteer staff
Family members of existing members anywhere in the US can qualify for membership
Offers multiple loan types: auto/student/home refinancing, personal, medical, repair, and business equipment
Loan application fees are low at $25–$50 non-refundable
cons: Membership is restricted to attendees of specific Jafari community centers in Houston, Dallas, and Austin — or qualifying family members
No cash handling, ATM access, debit/credit cards, or checking accounts — ACH transfers only
Small institution with only 365 members; loan maximums are capped at $35,000 for refinancing and $16,000 for business equipment
Monthly account fees of $3–$60 apply based on savings-to-loan balance ratios, replacing interest as the cost of membership
$200 required to open a savings account; cannot serve as a full-service primary bank for most consumers
best_for: Jafari Muslim community members in Houston, Dallas, or Austin seeking Sharia-compliant, interest-free loans
Existing members' family members anywhere in the US who want to refinance high-interest auto or student loans at 0%
Community members who need personal, medical, or home repair financing without paying interest
Individuals who want federally insured savings within an Islamic finance framework
diagnosis: JNICU is best suited for Jafari Muslim community members in Texas — or their family members nationwide — who want to eliminate interest payments on existing auto or student loans, or who need personal, medical, or business financing in a Sharia-compliant framework. The main caveat is that membership eligibility is tightly restricted to specific Jafari community center attendees and their families, and the credit union does not offer checking, ATM, or cash services, making it a supplement to — not a replacement for — a conventional bank account.
services: NCUA-insured savings accounts (up to $250,000 federal insurance)
Business equipment purchase or repair loans ($5,000–$16,000)
Voluntary/optional fee contributions to support credit union operations
Quarterly NCUA financial report access for member transparency
